# Tabellenexport

```{r reg01, include=F}
if(Sys.getenv("USERNAME") == "filse" ) .libPaths("D:/R-library4") 
```

## 

## `flextable`

## weitere Optionen

https://rfortherestofus.com/2019/11/how-to-make-beautiful-tables-in-r/

While **gt** is trying to do something different with its own interface, it may not suit your specific needs. Here is a listing of other leading table-making **R** packages, with links to their respective project pages:

-   `{knitr}` [link](https://yihui.org/knitr/)

-   `{kableExtra}` [link](https://haozhu233.github.io/kableExtra/)

-   `{gt}` [link](https://gt.rstudio.com/)

-   `{huxtable}` [link](https://hughjonesd.github.io/huxtable/)

-   `{DT}` [link](https://rstudio.github.io/DT/)

-   `{reactable}` [link](https://glin.github.io/reactable/)
